stem: An engineer takes 25 measurements of body weight using a bathroom scale. The measurements have a mean of 123.1 kg and a standard deviation of 15.1 kg. However, calibration tests reveal that a bathroom scale systematically underestimates the true value by between 2.2% and 5.4%. Estimate the true body weight and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 123.1 kg. After correcting for the 3.8% bias: ≈ 128.0 kg. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (2.2%-5.4%) and sampling variability: [119.9, 136.1]. Point estimate: 128.0 kg.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
